name: Northwestern Apricot Candy
recipeIngredient: 1/2 to 2/3 cup Chopped or slivered almonds, or chopped walnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove any stems or blemishes from the apricots.
2. Soak in warm water for 1 hour.
3. Cook slowly in the same water until very tender, stirring to avoid scorching.
4. Pass through a food mill or sieve.
5. Cook the apricot pulp in a 2-quart heavy saucepan until thick, stirring frequently to avoid scorching.
6. Mix sugar, cornstarch, and salt with the apricot pulp.
7. Cook until very thick, stirring constantly.
8. Add softened gelatin mixed with cold water. Stir until dissolved and cook until mixture is again thick.
9. Remove from heat and mix in almonds.
10. Turn into a 9 x 7 inches shallow glass dish that has been rinsed with cold water.
11. Let stand for 24 hours.
12. Cut the candy into rectangular pieces (about 60) and roll in confectioners sugar.
13. Let stand on a rack until the outside has dried (texture should be slightly chewy).
14. Store in a covered container. Makes about 1 pound, 12 ounces, depending on amount of nuts used.
